goal: A speed-first fact-check / question-answering skill: triage a question (tech, term, or open question) as simple vs complex, fan out parallel web searches, early-exit on corroboration, and return a citation-backed bottom-line answer within a hard wall-clock budget — simple <=2 min, complex <=5 min. Efficiency is the defining requirement.

stages.gate_evidence: spec parses; carries recommended_design/prioritized_actions/altitude/scorecard/handoff; altitude=full; verdict=draft (stub, required-at-full testing/tdd/metrics absent -> capped draft, allowed at G gate); 14 adversarial-checklist edges; 5 P0 actions A1-A5

E gate ok: ran=true; 21/21 cases pass; P0 5/5 done; checklist_coverage 14/14 (verbatim diff clean); harness re-run by conductor (node evals/run_all.mjs -> exit 0, 15/15 PASS). Real red log = 15 FAIL lines incl. idempotency+CLI. Mutation: complex source-bar 2->1 flipped D6 red, reverted green. B1 behavioral case run for real (parallel search, 0 fetch, early-exit) -> produced answer VALID via check_answer.mjs. SKILL.md 94 lines / ~1.3k tokens (lean).

Lossless SKIP. Smoke test all-Healthy (SKILL.md 94 lines / 1315 tokens; 22.9% always-loaded share short-circuited by absolute-size guard; 1 unified protocol). diff_lossless.py: 0 LOST / 0 REWRITTEN, 382 lines kept verbatim (exit 0). Retrigger: description ~262 tokens, slightly over 200 but anti-triggers (deep-research/album/hifi/opinion) are load-bearing -> kept deliberately. Harden: SKILL.md already concrete (per-tier hard caps). No lossless win; conservative skip per Stage Z policy.

Loopback from Final Acceptance: the 1st independent battery (41 CLI inputs) found 2 P0 validator bugs (E_UNSOURCED_CLAIM evadable via stray [n] in Sources/Caveats -> green-but-wrong; CRLF false-negative) + nits. Fixed: CRLF normalize, body-only citation scan, real-content BLUF, distinct-URL source bar, clean CLI errors; docs realigned. 5 regression cases added (D16-D20). Re-ran E gate: 26/26 pass, P0 5/5, checklist 14/14, harness re-run by conductor exit 0. New mutation: whole-text citation scan flips D17+D18 red, reverted green.

Re-audit of the BUILT skill: all 7 pillars present (14/14) -> re_audit_verdict=industrial. C1-C3 pass. C4: battery-2 (fresh subagent, 50 inputs) found the loop-1 traceability fix STILL defeatable by a [n] inside a URL, a code block, or a Caveats-before-Sources block -> battery_verdict=candidate -> loop back to Stage E.

Loop-2 fix: traceability is now REGION-BASED — citations counted only in the Answer + Key-evidence regions, with code spans/blocks and URLs stripped, and Caveats excluded by region (position-independent). Added 3 regression cases (D21-D23) for the exact battery-2 exploit shapes. Re-ran E gate: 29/29 pass, P0 5/5, checklist 14/14, harness re-run by conductor exit 0. New mutation: disabling the URL-strip flips D21 red (reverted). Verified the 3 exploit shapes now return INVALID and legit answers stay VALID.

Re-audit unchanged: all 7 pillars still present -> re_audit_verdict=industrial. C4 battery-3 (fresh subagent, 41 inputs) confirmed the region-based fix closed the whole substring-evasion class, but found 2 new P1s: a [n] inside an HTML comment still counted, and trivial-citation laundering (uncited Answer line passes if evidence cites trivia). HTML comment is structural -> loop back to E. Trivial laundering is irreducibly semantic.

Loop-3 fix: strip HTML comments from the citable region (D24 regression added, mutation-guarded). The semantic trivial-laundering case is NOT deterministically fixable (no structural rule separates uncited-summary+cited-trivia from a legit uncited-synthesis+cited-support without forcing mis-citation on inference answers — verified a synthesis answer stays VALID) -> docs realigned: validator=structural traceability, protocol(search-protocol.md s6)=semantic (which it addresses). Re-ran E gate: 30/30 pass, P0 5/5, checklist 14/14, harness re-run exit 0.

Re-audit unchanged: 7/7 pillars present -> re_audit_verdict=industrial. C4 battery-4 (fresh subagent, 44 inputs, FAIR mandate) confirmed the synthesis false-negative case is correctly VALID and the semantic layer is properly delegated to + handled by the protocol, but found 2 MORE structural invisible-marker vectors (a [n] hidden in an HTML attribute and in a markdown reference-link definition) -> battery_verdict=candidate. loops_taken=3=MAX_FULL_LOOPS -> STOP (no 4th discovery loop). The 2 vectors were remediated deterministically as a final hardening (strip HTML tags + ref-def lines; regressions D25/D26, mutation-guarded; 32/32 green), but per the anti-inflation rule industrial is NOT certifiable without a clean fresh battery, which the loop budget no longer allows -> effective_verdict honestly held at candidate.

battery_bugs: battery-1: E_UNSOURCED_CLAIM evadable via a stray [n] in the Sources list / Caveats (green-but-wrong); CRLF line endings false-negative — FIXED (D16-D20)
battery-2: traceability defeatable by a [n] inside a URL / code block / Caveats-before-Sources — FIXED via region-based citation scan (D21-D23)

battery-3: a [n] inside an HTML comment counted (FIXED, D24); trivial-citation laundering (uncited Answer + cited-trivia) — IRREDUCIBLY SEMANTIC, delegated to the protocol (search-protocol.md s6) and documented as the validator's scope
battery-4: a [n] inside an HTML attribute / a markdown reference-link definition counted — remediated deterministically (D25/D26) past the loop budget; NOT re-certified by a clean 5th battery

blocking_gaps: Independent battery not clean within the loop budget (MAX_FULL_LOOPS=3): battery-4 found 2 structural 'invisible-marker' citation-laundering vectors. Both are now FIXED deterministically (D25/D26, mutation-guarded, 32/32 green) but the fix was NOT re-certified by a fresh independent battery, so per the anti-inflation rule the verdict is held at candidate, not industrial.

The 'invisible-marker evasion' class (hiding a citation [n] in a non-rendering construct: HTML comment/attribute/tag, code, URL, ref-def) is an asymptote for a regex structural linter — each battery tends to find one more hiding spot. The validator is robust for realistic / good-faith answers (a model following the skill emits visible [n] citations; verified on a real end-to-end run and 26 deterministic cases); the residual vectors require DELIBERATE citation-hiding.

Trivial-citation laundering (an uncited Answer line passing because evidence bullets cite trivia) is irreducibly semantic — no structural rule separates it from a legitimate uncited-synthesis answer without forcing mis-citation on inference. It is correctly delegated to the protocol layer (search-protocol.md s6: cite the load-bearing claim, separate observation from inference), not the deterministic validator.
